articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: United States dismantles Uruguay to finish 2025 on high note
article.description: Sebastian Berhalter kicked off the scoring with a thunderous strike and Alex Freeman added a pair of first-half goals as the United States ran roughshod over Uruguay in a friendly at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida, on Tuesday night.
article.linkText: USMNT dismantles Uruguay to finish '25 on high

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (Mauricio Pochettino has tense interaction during post-match news conference) GLOBAL 20251119

article.story:
Sebastian Berhalter kicked off the scoring with a thunderous strike and Alex Freeman added a pair of first-half goals as the United States ran roughshod over Uruguay in a 5-1 friendly win at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida, on Tuesday night.

The U.S. took a surprising lead in the first half when Berhalter smashed a shot from a set piece past Uruguay keeper Cristopher Fiermarin.

And the surprises kept coming for the Americans, who were playing without some of their biggest stars like Christian Pulisic, Antonee Robinson, Weston McKennie and Tyler Adams.

Ten minutes later Freeman beat Fiermarin with a towering header from a corner kick for his first U.S. goal, and then scored again as he tiptoed through the Uruguay defense and rifled in a close-range shot to make it 3-0.

The goals kept coming for the U.S. as Diego Luna finished off a nice team move down the left flank with a first-time shot before Uruguay finally got on the scoreboard with a beautiful overhead kick from Giorgian de Arrascaeta that sent the visitors to the locker room trailing 4-1.

U.S. manager Mauricio Pochettino was irritated by the late goal when he spoke to TNT during an interview at halftime.

"We need to keep our intensity and our concentration," Pochettino said. "That can't happen. Now we need to control the emotions and control the second half. That is the most difficult thing."

Pochettino made nine changes to the lineup that beat Paraguay 2-1 on Saturday, with Sergiño Dest and goalkeeper Matthew Freese the only two players to make the XI again.

Giovanni Reyna, who scored in his first start for the U.S. since the summer of 2024, was one of those left out of the lineup, but he came on for Luna in the 61st minute with his team still leading 4-1.

Uruguay were down to 10 players shortly after Reyna took the field when Rodrigo Bentancur was shown a straight red card for a lunging slide tackle that caught Berhalter on his lower leg.

The Americans took advantage of the extra man as Reyna set up fellow substitute Tanner Tessmann for his first goal in a U.S. jersey to extend the lead to four goals.

The result marked the first time the U.S. has scored five goals against a team that has won a World Cup or any team from South America and helps Pochettino's side end a tumultuous calendar year on a major high.

The Americans have won four games, including three straight, during the five-game unbeaten streak against World Cup-bound teams.

Next year, the U.S. will play two friendlies in March -- likely against Portugal and Belgium -- and then two more in late May or early June ahead of hosting the World Cup on home soil along with Canada and Mexico next summer.

Information from The Associated Press was used in this recap.

videos.headline: Mauricio Pochettino has tense interaction during post-match news conference
videos.description: Mauricio Pochettino gets fired up during his news conference after the USMNT's win over Uruguay.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! USA 1, Uruguay 0. Sebastian Berhalter (USA) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Sergiño Dest following a set piece situation.
Goal! USA 2, Uruguay 0. Alexander Freeman (USA) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Sebastian Berhalter with a cross following a corner.
Goal! USA 3, Uruguay 0. Alexander Freeman (USA) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Auston Trusty.
Goal! USA 4, Uruguay 0. Diego Luna (USA) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner.
Goal! USA 4, Uruguay 1. Giorgian de Arrascaeta (Uruguay) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Rodrigo Zalazar with a headed pass.
First Half ends, USA 4, Uruguay 1.
Substitution, Uruguay. Joaquín Piquerez replaces Matías Viña.
Substitution, Uruguay. Rodrigo Aguirre replaces Federico Viñas.
Substitution, Uruguay. Brian Rodríguez replaces Rodrigo Zalazar.
Second Half begins USA 4, Uruguay 1.
Rodrigo Bentancur (Uruguay) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, USA. Folarin Balogun replaces Haji Wright.
Substitution, USA. Tanner Tessmann replaces Sergiño Dest.
Substitution, USA. Giovanni Reyna replaces Diego Luna.
Rodrigo Bentancur (Uruguay) is shown the red card.
Substitution, Uruguay. Emiliano Martínez replaces Giorgian de Arrascaeta.
Goal! USA 5, Uruguay 1. Tanner Tessmann (USA) header from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Giovanni Reyna with a cross following a corner.
Substitution, Uruguay. Facundo Torres replaces Maxi Araújo.
Giovanni Reyna (USA) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, USA. Max Arfsten replaces John Tolkin because of an injury.
Substitution, USA. Brenden Aaronson replaces Timothy Tillman.
Folarin Balogun (USA) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Rodrigo Aguirre (Uruguay) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, USA. Cristian Roldan replaces Aidan Morris.
Second Half ends, USA 5, Uruguay 1.
